DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Matt Metzger of Belvedere Legal (415-513-5980) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Opallios LLC

Attorney, Nathaniel P. Bualat of Crowell & Moring LLP (415-986-2800) appears as counsel for all defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as reflected in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling With modification.

DEFENDANTS SUSAN DUBMAN, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA and LAURA ESSERMAN, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. Defendants' request for judicial notice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Defendant's demurrer to the first though fourth causes of action based on absolute immunity is overruled because the argument could have been made by demurrer to the FAC and, thus, Defendants are barred from raising the argument at this time. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.41, subd. (b).) Contrary to Defendants' claim in all relevant respects the misrepresentation claims in the FAC and the SAC are materially identical, including the "agency" allegations, and thus the rule of section 430.41(b) governs. Defendants' demurrer to the first though fourth causes of action based on Plaintiff's failure to allege facts to overcome the limited waiver of immunity in Gov. Code section 822.2 is sustained with leave to amend. With particularity, Plaintiff must plead that Esserman and Dubman acted with "actual fraud, corruption or actual malice," or hatred or ill will toward the Plaintiff. (See Gov. Code, 822.2; Masters v. San Bernardino County Employees Ret. Assn. (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 30, 42; Schonfeld v. City of Vallejo (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 401, 409 & fn. 9.) The SAC contains allegations of non-disclosures and perhaps misrepresentations, but not with particularity actual fraud, corruption or actual malice. Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 311 is inapposite and does not relieve Plaintiff in this case from pleading with particularity the facts necessary to meet the limited statutory waiver of immunity. Plaintiff is granted 30 days leave to amend. Defendants shall submit a proposed order consistent with this tentative. The parties may appear ex parte to seek extension of discovery deadline if necessary. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745, email: lmartinez@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matt Metzger of Belvedere Legal telephone number 415-513-5980 for Plaintiff Opallios, LLC.
Nathaniel Bualat of Crowell Moring LLP telephone number 415-986-2800 for Defendants Susan Dubman, Laura Esserman, M.D. and The Regents of The University of California.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUSAN DUBMAN, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA and LAURA ESSERMAN, M.D'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and denied in part (see order for full text).
